changes.value:
## What

Create a new `/lsp` skill that agents use to diagnose and fix LSP server issues. When a user types `/lsp` (or the statusline says "/lsp to fix"), the agent:

1. Calls `code_context` with `op: "lsp status"` to get current state
2. Shows the user which languages are detected and which LSPs are missing
3. For each missing LSP with an `install_hint`, offers to run the install command
4. After installation, re-checks status to confirm the fix

### Files to create:
- `builtin/skills/lsp/SKILL.md` — skill definition with frontmatter and instructions

### SKILL.md should instruct the agent to:
- Always start by calling `code_context` with `op: "lsp status"`
- Present a clear table: language icon, server name, status (installed/missing), install command
- Ask the user before running install commands (they may involve npm/pip/brew)
- After installing, re-run `lsp status` to confirm
- If all healthy, report success

### Skill frontmatter:
```yaml
---
name: lsp
description: Diagnose and install missing LSP servers for your project
metadata:
  author: swissarmyhammer
  version: "1.0"
---
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `builtin/skills/lsp/SKILL.md` exists with valid frontmatter
- [ ] Skill is deployable via `sah init` (skill deployment picks up new builtin skills automatically)
- [ ] Agent can invoke `/lsp` and get actionable LSP status
- [ ] Agent runs install commands via shell skill when user approves
- [ ] Agent re-checks status after install to confirm fix

## Tests
- [ ] `sah init --scope project` deploys the lsp skill
- [ ] Manual: invoke `/lsp` in Claude Code, verify it calls `lsp status` and presents findings
